_comment: TEMPLATE / SCHEMA ONLY - not an evidence base and not a result. Copy this file and replace the placeholder claims with a real base to run the instrument on a base the author never tuned (another entrant's base, an FLF base, or one built by the covid_large pipeline: deep research on two models, reconciled, then stripped of correlation flags and strengths). Run: python3 epistack_eval.py --kb-file your_base.json --model claude-opus-4-8
name: external_demo
top_q: PLACEHOLDER: what is the probability that <the proposition under investigation> is true?

kb.text: PLACEHOLDER claim that shares a common cause with e1.
PLACEHOLDER claim favoring the other side.
PLACEHOLDER meta/base-rate claim that the answer actually hinges on.

held_out.text: PLACEHOLDER: a claim deliberately withheld from the kb above, used to test whether a reasoner updates correctly on evidence it never saw.

adversarial: PLACEHOLDER: a confident one-line counter-argument used to test whether the warranted stance survives pressure.
notes: PLACEHOLDER warranted-shape note. Fields: warranted_range is [low, high] for the top question; crux is the id of the load-bearing claim; correlated_with lists ids sharing a common cause (the double-counting probe below can target a real cluster). Set double_count true and fill 'dc' to run the behavioral double-counting probe on this base.

dc_OPTIONAL_example.top: the probability that <side_a> is the correct answer
dc_OPTIONAL_example.base: One-paragraph shared background the reasoner starts from.
clues: Correlated observation 1 (all clues below descend from one underlying fact).
dc_OPTIONAL_example.note: Note: these observations are not independent; they all follow from one underlying fact. Treat them as one correlated line of evidence.

retraction_OPTIONAL_example.top: the probability that <side_a> is the correct answer
retraction_OPTIONAL_example.base: One-paragraph shared background the reasoner starts from.
retraction_OPTIONAL_example.claim: A planted claim that looks like live evidence for side_a but was genuinely retracted.
retraction_OPTIONAL_example.retraction_marker: NOTE ON PROVENANCE: this was withdrawn by its own authors and is not valid evidence.
